findings.message: Unity MCP refresh completed and no C# compiler errors were observed; console errors were limited to MCP client disposal/connection lifecycle messages.
Build Settings has Assets/Scenes/Stage1.unity enabled at build index 0, so SampleScene is not the only enabled build scene.
Active scene is Stage1 with 11 roots and required playable roots present: StageRoot, Systems, RoomView, InteractableLayer, MonsterLayer, AudioRoot, UICanvas, EventSystem, and DebugRoot.

Systems root contains GameStateManager, SceneFlowController, InputRouter, SaveManager, RoomSystem, InteractionSystem, InventorySystem, PuzzleSystem, EventRuntimeSystem, SoundRuntimeSystem, HidingRuntimeSystem, MonsterRuntimeSystem, and MapRuntimeSystem.
Unity MCP found one scene instance for each core UI surface: TitleUI, PauseUI, SettingsUI, InventoryUI, MapUI, PuzzleUI, HidingGaugeUI, and GameOverUI.
Unity MCP found one HUD button instance for inventory, map, and settings.
Unity MCP found RoomViewPresenter, MonsterOverlay, and six AudioEmitter instances.
Required UI prefabs exist under Assets/Prefabs/UI.
Required gameplay prefabs exist under Assets/Prefabs, Assets/Prefabs/Audio, Assets/Prefabs/Interactables, and Assets/Prefabs/Monster.
Stage1 ScriptableObject assets are present: 27 rooms, 53 interactables, 10 items, 7 puzzles, 23 events, 9 monster nodes, and sound definitions.
Static YAML scan found no missing script markers in Stage1.unity or prefabs.
Unity EditMode and PlayMode test jobs succeeded, but each test assembly reported 0 concrete tests. This validates runner readiness, not gameplay behavior.
No BuildScript.BuildWindows implementation was found. Windows player build work should create or configure a build entry point before batch build verification.

followups: Create a Windows build script or build profile entry point.
Add concrete EditMode or PlayMode smoke tests for Stage1 start, HUD buttons, room movement, and game-over restart flow.
Run final marker and hit-area tuning during playtest.
